message:
feat(core,parser,planner,vdbe,func): scratch-reused parsing, UDF-gen invalidation, extracted vacuum/pragma maintenance

Wires the primitives from the previous commit (cursor position stamps,
page-cache efficiency snapshots) through the full statement lifecycle
and adds a family of related query-pipeline changes that have been
accumulating on this branch.

fsqlite-parser
--------------
* Introduces `StatementParseScratch`, a reusable scratch allocator that
  lets repeat callers (the connection's prepared-statement path in
  particular) amortize per-parse allocations. Exposed through three new
  entry points — `parse_statements_with_scratch`,
  `parse_single_statement_with_scratch`, and a shared internal helper —
  all re-exported from the crate root.
* Lexer and parser extensions needed to support the scratch-reusing
  path without changing behaviour for one-shot callers.
* Semantic resolver picks up additional `FunctionArity` handling and a
  round of hardening around UDF lookups so that prepared plans can be
  invalidated cheaply when the function registry is bumped.

fsqlite-planner
---------------
* Codegen wiring for the new cursor position-stamp plumbing and the UDF
  registry generation that the VDBE now checks on dispatch. Public
  surface grows a few accessor methods to let higher layers observe
  planner decisions without cloning the planner state.

fsqlite-vdbe
------------
* Engine integrates `CursorPositionStamp` into its reuse-trace so that
  stale statement handles can detect cursor movement across suspends.
* New `last_position_stamp` tracking on cursor wrappers, with fast-path
  guards that avoid any recomputation when the stamp is unchanged.
* Dispatch checks `function_registry_generation` before firing UDFs so
  that redefined functions always miss the plan cache.

fsqlite-func
------------
* `builtins.rs` grows the new built-in entries the planner/VDBE changes
  expect and updates a handful of existing ones to use the current
  `FunctionArity` shape.

fsqlite-core
------------
* `connection.rs` grows `function_registry_generation` on the
  `PreparedStatementTemplate` / `PreparedStatement` and their clone
  paths, along with a new `statement_parse_scratch` RefCell so prepared
  paths reuse parser allocations. Prepared-DML dispatch now fails fast
  with a structured reuse-event when the registry generation has
  advanced, closing a correctness gap on UDF redefinition.
* Removes the now-unused `PagerBackend::copy_database_to` shim — the
  only caller moved into the new `vacuum` module below.
* `compat_persist.rs` picks up the extra master-entry plumbing used by
  the vacuum rebuild path.
* New `vacuum` submodule (`src/vacuum.rs`, wired in `lib.rs`) owns the
  non-wasm compacting persist pipeline. It centralises temp-rebuild ID
  allocation behind a crate-local atomic counter and exposes
  `persist_compacted_database` to the rest of the core crate. Keeps
  VACUUM-specific branches out of the hot `connection.rs`.
* New `connection/pragma_maintenance` submodule (`src/connection/pragma_maintenance.rs`)
  extracts `pragma_integrity_check_rows` and `pragma_wal_checkpoint_rows`
  out of the connection monolith. The checkpoint path preserves the
  SQLite-sentinel behaviour when not in WAL mode, short-circuits when
  blocked by concurrent txns, invalidates cached read/write txns, and
  feeds the checkpoint advisor with wall-clock duration deltas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
